Figure 11: Error distributions between PINNs output and exact solution of Example 3 at the terminal time. Left: The horizontal velocity u; Middle: the vertical velocity v; Right: the pressure p; Upper: outside the moving interface; Lower: inside the moving interface.

Figure 9: Training points distribution separated by a solution-driven interface tracked along the training epoches and classified as internal (red) and external (blue) subsets, where projections of interface onto the xy-plane, z = t ∈ {0.01, 0.12, 0.23, 0.34, 0.45, 0.56, 0.67, 0.78, 0.89, 1.00} are plotted as red curves. (a) In the beginning of training, the interface is initially set as a circular cylinder; (b) in the middle of training, the interface keep deforming due to the solution-driven mechanism by the PINNs output; (c) at the end of training, the interface is finally deformed as an irregular shape due to the dependency of PINNs output.

Figure 10: PINNs outputs of Example 3 at the terminal time. Left: The horizontal velocity u; Middle: the vertical velocity v; Right: the pressure p; Upper: outside the moving interface; Lower: inside the moving interface.
